head: 44c1476

verified_addressed.request: Move detailed routing docs out; keep only general matcher syntax on this page.
verified_addressed.verification: Current matcher.mdx is now a syntax-and-fields reference (Cache Policy Examples, Failsafe Policy Examples, Pattern Syntax sections). Detailed routing prose has been removed.

verified_addressed.request: Drop ConfigMatcher instance-based design; expose flat matchers.Match(cfg, req, resp); minimize per-request allocation.
verified_addressed.verification: matchers/matcher.go now exposes package-level Match(ctx, configs, req, resp), MatchConfig(...), MatchParams(...). No ConfigMatcher type. Callers use matchers.Match(ctx, fe.config.Matchers, req, nil) (erpc/networks.go:262, upstream/upstream.go:298).

verified_addressed.request: Match must accept real ctx, not nil — nil panics in prod.
verified_addressed.verification: Match signature is Match(ctx context.Context, configs []*common.MatcherConfig, req *common.NormalizedRequest, resp *common.NormalizedResponse). Internally finality := req.Finality(ctx). Both call sites pass ctx through.

verified_addressed.request: DataFinalityStateSlice wrapper unnecessary; native []DataFinalityState should YAML-unmarshal fine.
verified_addressed.verification: DataFinalityStateSlice is removed. Repo-wide grep finds no references. MatcherConfig.Finality is plain []DataFinalityState.
Field declared as Finality []DataFinalityState `yaml:"finality,omitempty" json:"finality" tstype:"DataFinalityState[]"`. Suggestion applied verbatim.

verified_addressed.request: Pass ctx into IsResultEmptyish/IsObjectNull for tracing; tighten nil-resp guard.
verified_addressed.verification: if resp != nil { isEmptyish = resp.IsObjectNull(ctx) || resp.IsResultEmptyish(ctx) }. ctx threaded through. Note: Aram's literal suggestion (`!resp.IsObjectNull() && !resp.IsResultEmptyish(ctx)`) was logically inverted (would compute isNotEmpty); current code matches the intent (true when response is empty/null) and is correct.

verified_addressed.request: Defaulting algorithm for Failsafe is unintuitive / hasUpstreamPolicies always-true bug. Suggested: 'if any user policies are defined, do not apply defaults at all'.
verified_addressed.verification: matchFailsafeConfigs() and the per-method/finality merging loop are GONE (commit 0acefc5: 'remove legacy failsafe config matching function'). UpstreamConfig.SetDefaults now does literally `if len(u.Failsafe) > 0 { keep as-is } else if defaults present { copy whole list }`. An in-code comment at line 1675-1677 explicitly references review comments #11/#12 acknowledging the deliberate design change.

verified_addressed.request: 'What does this mean exactly?' on the comment 'Do not inherit Failsafe defaults into overrides; overrides should replace them.'
verified_addressed.verification: Both the ambiguous comment AND the code it described have been removed. ProviderConfig.SetDefaults (line 1542) now simply iterates p.Overrides and calls override.SetDefaults(upsDefaults), and override.SetDefaults applies the all-or-nothing rule from the resolution above. No need for a clarifying comment.
FailsafeConfig.Copy() now deep-copies both Params (line 1118-1121) and Finality (line 1122-1126) for every matcher. Suggestion applied.

verified_addressed.request: Two concerns: (a) stop handling legacy fields inside cache_policy logic — fully migrate to matchers; (b) EmptyState should come from the exact matcher that matched the request, not a hand-picked include matcher.
verified_addressed.verification: (a) NewCachePolicy now calls cfg.SetDefaults() up front (cache_policy.go:36) which converts legacy fields once via convertLegacyMatchers, and the policy code below operates only on Matchers. The legacy-detection branch in EmptyState() is gone. (b) New PolicyWithMatcher type (cache_policy.go:21-30) carries the specific matcher that matched, and EmptyState() returns pm.Matcher.Empty. MatchesForGetWithMatcher / MatchesForSetWithMatcher return the matched matcher; EvmJsonRpcCache plumbs PolicyWithMatcher through (architecture/evm/json_rpc_cache.go:203, 282-283, 749-792). Test coverage added at data/cache_policy_test.go:228.

findings.title: GATING: 8-10k rps matcher benchmark requested by reviewer is still missing
findings.original_quote: We need to test and benchmark this carefully to ensure it won't impact prod as it's basically run on every single request and some tests are needed with a load of like 8k-10k rps with a normal compute instance.
findings.evidence: Glob `matchers/*` returns only matcher.go — no matcher_test.go and no matcher_bench_test.go exist. Repo-wide search for `BenchmarkMatch|BenchmarkMatcher` returns zero results. Existing benchmarks under erpc/networks_bench_test.go (BenchmarkNetworkForward_*) exercise full forward paths but do not isolate the matcher hot path nor target the 8-10k rps scenario Aram requested. No micro-benchmark of matchers.Match / matchers.MatchConfig / matchers.MatchParams exists. Since aramalipoor's last comment (2025-10-17), the only matcher-related commits are the Oct 2025 PolicyWithMatcher refactor and the May 2026 main-branch merge; no benchmark infrastructure was added.
findings.rationale: This is the explicit gating condition the reviewer set on merging. Per-request allocations in matchers.Match are non-trivial: each call still does (i) JsonRpcRequest() lookup, (ii) NetworkId() string copy, (iii) reverse iteration over configs with type-switch in matchParam, (iv) potential WildcardMatch invocations per field. Without numbers, the merge gate is unmet.
findings.action: Add matchers/matcher_bench_test.go with at least: (a) BenchmarkMatch_Hit_MethodOnly (single matcher, '*' method) and (b) BenchmarkMatch_Hit_AllFields (network + method + 2-elt finality + 2-elt params). Run on a normal instance with `b.RunParallel` and document ns/op + allocs/op. Optionally add BenchmarkMatch_Miss to confirm fast-fail cost. Then ping aramalipoor for the requested fresh look.

findings.title: Cursor bot HIGH: legacy `finality` and `empty` cache fields silently dropped
findings.original_quote: The hasLegacyFields check in convertLegacyMatchers for CachePolicyConfig only inspects Network, Method, and Params. If a user configures only `finality: realtime` or `empty: allow` without setting network/method/params, hasLegacyFields evaluates to false, the finality/empty constraints are silently dropped, and a default catch-all include matcher is created instead.
findings.evidence: common/defaults.go:622 reads `hasLegacyFields := c.Network != "" || c.Method != "" || len(c.Params) > 0`. CachePolicyConfig.Finality is DataFinalityState (int) and Empty is CacheEmptyBehavior (int) — declared at common/config.go:261-262. With only `finality: realtime` set (=2) and Network/Method/Params unset, hasLegacyFields is false → control falls to the `if len(c.Matchers) == 0` branch at line 660 which appends `&MatcherConfig{Method: "*", Action: MatcherInclude}` with no Finality and no Empty. The user's realtime-only intent is silently lost. Same path triggers for `empty: allow` and `empty: only`. Confirmed by reading the code; the cursor-bot finding is accurate.
findings.rationale: Silent constraint loss in cache policies can produce data correctness regressions (caching non-realtime data into a realtime-only policy, or caching empty responses where the user explicitly set `empty: ignore`/`only`).
findings.action: Tighten the gate. Two viable fixes: (1) extend hasLegacyFields to include `c.Finality != DataFinalityStateFinalized || c.Empty != CacheEmptyBehaviorIgnore` (zero-value compromise: explicit 'finalized'/'ignore' still won't be detected, but those are the safe defaults so the loss is benign); or (2) better, change CachePolicyConfig.Finality to *DataFinalityState and Empty to *CacheEmptyBehavior so explicit-vs-unset is distinguishable. Add a config_test.go case that loads YAML containing only `finality: realtime` and asserts the resulting matcher has Finality=[realtime]. Same for `empty: only`.

findings.title: Cursor bot MEDIUM: fallback executor at networks.go:287 may override an exclude-only matcher (intentional but undocumented)
findings.original_quote: The new fallback at the end of getFailsafeExecutor unconditionally returns the last executor when no match is found. ... If a user's matchers intentionally exclude certain requests, the fallback still returns the last executor (default catch-all), effectively overriding the exclusion.
findings.evidence: erpc/networks_registry.go:148-156 always appends a synthetic default executor with `config: nil, method: "*"`. Because that synthetic executor has no matchers, it falls into the legacy-matching branch (networks.go:274-283) and always matches, so the fallback at networks.go:287-289 is effectively unreachable in normal operation. The bot's concern collapses to: 'a user who writes only an exclude matcher still gets the synthetic default failsafe.' That is the system's intended behavior — failsafe must always apply something to avoid bare-metal upstream calls.
findings.rationale: Surfacing because the orchestrator triaged this as 'disagree, intentional design' and I confirm. Closing as not-a-bug, but flagging that the current code reads ambiguously: the explicit fallback at lines 287-289 looks like a safety net but is dead because the synthetic default catches everything first. Worth a one-line comment.
findings.action: Either remove the dead fallback (lines 286-289) since networks_registry.go guarantees a synthetic default executor, OR add a comment explaining 'The synthetic default failsafe appended in networks_registry.go ensures we always reach a match in the loop above; this fallback is defensive in case that invariant is ever broken.' Reply to the bot thread linking to networks_registry.go:148-156 to close it.

residual_risks: Hot-path performance: matchers.Match runs on every request in two places (erpc/networks.go:262 for failsafe selection, upstream/upstream.go:298 for per-upstream failsafe). It still does at least one type-switch per param plus WildcardMatch per network/method/param string, all under reverse iteration. Until benchmark numbers exist, the 8-10k rps gate remains unmet (see pr388-prev-001).

Implicit API behavior change: the new `if len(u.Failsafe) > 0 { keep as-is }` semantics are correct per the prior review consensus, but they are a behavior change from the prior per-method merging. Any existing user config that relied on partial-merge (e.g., upstreamDefaults provides Retry, upstream provides Hedge expecting both to apply) will silently lose the inherited policy. The in-code comment at common/defaults.go:1675-1677 acknowledges this; consider adding a CHANGELOG/migration note.

Empty/finality zero-value ambiguity: even after pr388-prev-002 is fixed via the int-comparison heuristic, users cannot explicitly opt into `finality: finalized` or `empty: ignore` and have it survive convertLegacyMatchers without also setting another legacy field. The pointer-based fix avoids this entirely.
testing_gaps: matchers/ has no test file at all (matcher_test.go does not exist). MatchConfig, MatchParams, matchParam, paramToString, and Match are exercised only indirectly through cache_policy_test.go and failsafe_test.go. Add direct unit tests with table-driven cases covering: nil resp, empty configs, exclude-only configs, multi-matcher precedence (last-match-wins), wildcard edge cases, and numeric-comparison patterns in params.

No regression test for the cursor-bot pr388-prev-002 case: load a YAML cache policy with only `finality: realtime` (no method/network/params) and assert the resulting matcher carries the finality constraint.
No micro-benchmark for matchers.Match (gating per pr388-prev-001).
